POL.Name: neg_et
POL.Comment: DEF: Recording negative income to zero

POL.Name: lma_et
POL.Comment: DEF: Select individuals that transition to unemployment during COVID, amend incomes accordingly

POL.Name: bed01_et
POL.Comment: BEN: School feeding, KG-Grade 8 students (in kind)

POL.Name: bed02_et
POL.Comment: BEN: School uniform, KG-Grade 12 students (in kind)

POL.Name: xhhadj_et
POL.Comment: INC: Adjust consumption to new disposable income

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Disposable income without imputed home produce

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Pension benefits ("Pension benefits" in Statistics Presenter)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Post-fiscal disposable income without imputed home produce

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Disposable income accounting for in-kind benefits

FUN.Name: SchedCalc
FUN.Comment: Tax Schedule - Liability on employment income

FUN.Name: SchedCalc
FUN.Comment: Tax Schedule - Liability on business profit income

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Downward adjustment of benefit take-ups based of external data

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Downward adjustment of benefit take-ups based of external data

FUN.Name: ArithOp
FUN.Comment: National Absolute poverty line, post fiscal

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Social security contributions ("SSC (employer and employee)" in Statistics Presenter)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Indirect taxes (“part of indirect taxes minus indirect benefits” in Statistics Presenter)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Disposable income with imputed own consumption(feeds into Statistics Presenter, do not change)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Simulated Consumption (needed for Statistics Presenter - do not change)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Post-fiscal simulated consumption (needed for Statistics Presenter - do not change)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Post-fiscal income accounting for home produce if appropiate (needed for Statistics Presenter - do not change)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: All employee/self-employed National Insurance Contributions (Statistics Presenter)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Agricultural benefits ("Agricultural benefits" in Statistics Presenter)

FUN.Name: DefIl
FUN.Comment: Indirect benefits (“part of indirect taxes minus indirect benefits” in Statistics Presenter)

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Downward adjustment of benefit take-ups based of external data

FUN.Name: ArithOp
FUN.Comment: Nationally defined equivalence scale (ses), per-capita equivalence scale(o ses01), or square root equivalence scale (ses02)

FUN.Name: ArithOp
FUN.Comment: Recording negative employment income to zero

FUN.Name: ArithOp
FUN.Comment: Recording negative Self-employment income to zero

FUN.Name: ArithOp
FUN.Comment: Recording negative farmer income to zero

FUN.Name: DefVar
FUN.Comment: Define temporary variables: random number, variable 'lma' defining labour market transitions, etc.

FUN.Name: DefConst
FUN.Comment: Specify 'transition shares' (shares of employed workers transitioning to unemployment across sectors)

FUN.Name: Elig
FUN.Comment: Set eligibility criteria for labour market transitions

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Remove the industry value if the above criteria are not met

FUN.Name: BenCalc
FUN.Comment: Random allocation of workers into unemployment (requires that random number < transition share for the relevant sector)

FUN.Name: DefConst
FUN.Comment: Specify share of own-account production consumed that is protected from changes.

PAR.Name: ils_dispy
PAR.Comment: Disposable income not accounting for own produce (as above)

PAR.Name: Def_Factor
PAR.Comment: Default value for all monetary variables

PAR.Name: x053
PAR.Comment: The expenditure of the household on kitchen items

PAR.Name: x052
PAR.Comment: The expenditure of the househod on Linens

PAR.Name: x0453
PAR.Comment: The expenditure of the household on kerosene

PAR.Name: x031
PAR.Comment: The expenditure of the household on clothshoemen,clothshoewomen,clothshoeboys,cl

PAR.Name: x0115
PAR.Comment: The sum of expenditures on oil

PAR.Name: x021
PAR.Comment: The household's expenditure on alcoholic drinks

PAR.Name: x0126
PAR.Comment: The hosuehold's expendture on non alcohoic drinks

PAR.Name: x0122
PAR.Comment: The sum of expenditures on coffee, Chat, and cigarettes

PAR.Name: x01199
PAR.Comment: The expenditure of the household other food items

PAR.Name: x0118
PAR.Comment: The expenditure of the household on sugar and salt

PAR.Name: x0117
PAR.Comment: The sum of expenditures on vegetables consumed at home and outside the home

PAR.Name: x0116
PAR.Comment: The sum of expenditures on fruit consumed at home and outside the home

PAR.Name: x0114
PAR.Comment: The expenditure of the household on milk, cheese, eggs, and other dairy products

PAR.Name: x0112
PAR.Comment: The expenditure of the household on meat

PAR.Name: x0111
PAR.Comment: The sum of expenditures on cereals, pulses, and tubers

PAR.Name: x0511
PAR.Comment: The expenditure of the household on furniture

PAR.Name: x0454
PAR.Comment: The expenditure of the household on charcol and firewood

PAR.Name: x0561
PAR.Comment: The expenditure of the household on battries, candels, and matches, and lamp

PAR.Name: x05621
PAR.Comment: The expenditure of the household on Domestic services by paid staff

PAR.Name: x073
PAR.Comment: The expenditure of the household on transport

PAR.Name: x111
PAR.Comment: The amount of expenditure of the household on its consumption of meals and snack

PAR.Name: x131
PAR.Comment: The expenditure of the household on laundary soap, andsoap, and other personal c

PAR.Name: $it_rate2
PAR.Comment: Income tax applicable to the second bracket

PAR.Name: $it_rate1
PAR.Comment: Income tax applicable to the first bracket

PAR.Name: $it_rate3
PAR.Comment: Income tax applicable to the third bracket

PAR.Name: $it_rate4
PAR.Comment: Income tax applicable to the fourth bracket

PAR.Name: $it_rate5
PAR.Comment: Income tax applicable to the fifth bracket

PAR.Name: $it_rate6
PAR.Comment: Income tax applicable to the six bracket

PAR.Name: $it_rate7
PAR.Comment: Income tax applicabe to the seventh bracket

PAR.Name: Band_UpLim
PAR.Comment: Uper limit of first income tax bracket

PAR.Name: Band_UpLim
PAR.Comment: Uper limit of second income tax bracket

PAR.Name: Band_UpLim
PAR.Comment: Uper limit of third income tax bracket

PAR.Name: Band_UpLim
PAR.Comment: Uper limit of fourth income tax bracket

PAR.Name: Band_UpLim
PAR.Comment: Uper limit of fifth income tax bracket

PAR.Name: Band_UpLim
PAR.Comment: Uper limit of sixth income tax bracket

PAR.Name: Base
PAR.Comment: Business profit income minus Employer pension contribution

PAR.Name: Band_UpLim
PAR.Comment: Uper limit of first income tax bracket

PAR.Name: Band_UpLim
PAR.Comment: Uper limit of second income tax bracket

PAR.Name: Band_UpLim
PAR.Comment: Uper limit of third income tax bracket

PAR.Name: Band_UpLim
PAR.Comment: Uper limit of fourth income tax bracket

PAR.Name: Band_UpLim
PAR.Comment: Uper limit of fifth income tax bracket

PAR.Name: Band_UpLim
PAR.Comment: Uper limit of sixth income tax bracket

PAR.Name: Output_Var
PAR.Comment: Tax from empoyment in formal sector

PAR.Name: Output_Var
PAR.Comment: Tax from Self-employment in formal sector

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: if annual turnover is greater than upper limit if presumptive tax

PAR.Name: Elig_Cond
PAR.Comment: Positive employment income and being in the formal sector

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: If 4 or less entitled person within eligible household

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: ... calculate average monthly (five days in a month) amount per eligible person. a daily benefit amount per individual become 90 ETB starting 2021 for four days in a month (A monthly benefit amount per individual become 315 ETB starting 2021 (adjusted for inflation for remaining years)

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: ... calculate average monthly (five days in a month) amount for 4 people in total. a daily benefit amount per individual become 90 ETB starting 2021 for four days in a month A monthly benefit amount per individual become 315 ETB starting 2021 (adjusted for inflation for remaining years)

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: If more than 4 people eligible within eligible household.

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: working age and with out disability

PAR.Name: urban_PW_hh
PAR.Comment: Variable with random numbers between 0 and 1

PAR.Name: Output_Var
PAR.Comment: Generate a random number between 0 and 1

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Matching the number of beneficiaries from the simulation with an external source by limiting the set of individuals whose random number is less than the actual ratio of those receiving benefits based on external data. Due to data limitations, we apply the same ratio specific to 2022 for all policy systems.

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Poor household residing in selected urban area

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Positive employment income and being in the military and policy force

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Positive employment income and being in the formal sector, not in the military and policy force

PAR.Name: #_Amount
PAR.Comment: 25 % pension contribution by employer for armed forces

PAR.Name: #_Amount
PAR.Comment: 11 % pension contribution by employer for other groups

PAR.Name: Formula
PAR.Comment: Calculate the amount of TOT for goods and services

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Monthly tap water consumption above 15 cubic meters

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: If entitled person within eligible households, who is not a client of public work component of urban PSNP

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: A monthly benefit amount per individual become 315 ETB starting 2021 (adjusted for inflation for remaining years)

PAR.Name: urban_DS_hh
PAR.Comment: Variable with random numbers between 0 and 1

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Matching the number of beneficiaries from the simulation with an external source by limiting the set of individuals whose random number is less than the actual ratio of those receiving benefits based on external data. Due to data limitations, we apply the same ratio specific to 2022 for all policy systems.

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Poor households living in selected urban area

PAR.Name: xhh_s
PAR.Comment: Consumption adjusted by difference between disposable income in current and base year

PAR.Name: ils_dispyx
PAR.Comment: Disposable income with Imputed value of own produce

PAR.Name: x0561
PAR.Comment: Expenditure: light (batteies, candels, matches, and lamp)

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: If 5 or more entitled person within eligible household.

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: 15 kg cereal multiplied by the average market price for wheat, maiz and braley and 4 kg of pulse multiplied by market price of haricot beans. Starting 2021, the monthly benefit amount become 15 kg wheat per individual (its monetary value is adjusted for inflation for remaining years). A client clients receives the transfer for six months in a year.

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: 15 kg cereal multiplied by the average market price for wheat, maiz and braley and 4 kg of pulse multiplied by market price of haricot beans. Starting 2021, the monthly benefit amount become 15 kg wheat per individual (its monetary value is adjusted for inflation for remaining years). A client clients receives the transfer for six months in a year.

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: If less than 5 entitled person within eligible household.

PAR.Name: rank_ru_pw
PAR.Comment: Variable with random numbers between 0 and 1

PAR.Name: xhh_ind
PAR.Comment: Variable with random numbers between 0 and 1 (for 2014 & 2015)

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Matching the number of beneficiaries from the simulation with an external source by limiting the set of individuals whose random number is less than the actual ratio of those receiving benefits based on external data. Due to data limitations, we apply the same ratio specific to 2022 for all policy systems.

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Poor households living in rural area

PAR.Name: rank_ru_ds
PAR.Comment: Variable with random numbers between 0 and 1

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: If 5 or more entitled person within eligible household

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: 15 kg cereal multiplied by the average market price for wheat, maiz and braley and 4 kg of pulse multiplied by market price of haricot beans. Starting 2021, the monthly benefit amount become 15 kg wheat per individual (its monetary value is adjusted for inflation for remaining years). A client clients receives the transfer for twelve months in a year.

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: 15 kg cereal multiplied by the average market price for wheat, maiz and braley and 4 kg of pulse multiplied by market price of haricot beans. Starting 2021, the monthly benefit amount become 15 kg wheat per individual (its monetary value is adjusted for inflation for remaining years). A client clients receives the transfer for twelve months in a year.

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: If less than 5 entitled person within eligible household

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Poor households living in rural area

PAR.Name: Formula
PAR.Comment: User can set ses01 or ses01

PAR.Name: i_lma_elig
PAR.Comment: Variable denoting eligibility for labour market transitions

PAR.Name: Elig_Cond
PAR.Comment: To have an industry value and to be eligible for a transition, les (labour market status) has to be equal to 1, 2 or 3 (employee or farmer) and non-farm earnings must be positive in sectors other than agriculture. In agriculture, those (household heads) with positive farm income may only transition if they have other sources of earnings and are in the formal sector.

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: If the individual meets these conditions...

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: ...industry value is retained (the individual is eligible for transition)

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: ...industry value is set to -1 (unknown; the individual is not eligible for transition)

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: If the individual does not meet these conditions...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: No change in labour market status...

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: For students in goverment owned school in Addis Ababa (KG-8 GRADE)

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: Value of food ration per labour/school day (i.e. 5 days per week) from September upto December 2020

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: For students in goverment owned school in Addis Ababa (KG)

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: A unit cost of school Uniform

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: For students in goverment owned school in Addis Ababa (9-12 GRADE)

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: For students in goverment owned school in Addis Ababa (7-8 GRADE)

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: For students in goverment owned school in Addis Ababa (4-6 GRADE)

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: For students in goverment owned school in Addis Ababa (1-3 GRADE)

PAR.Name: $protect_cons
PAR.Comment: An optional constant for the share of own-account production consumed that is protected from the shock. This is ca. 25% in Sub-Saharan Africa, but can be set to 0%.

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: Disposable income higher than in base year

PAR.Name: Comp_perTU
PAR.Comment: … increases by change in disposable income

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: … decreases by change in disposable income

PAR.Name: x0561
PAR.Comment: EXPENDITURE : light (batteies, candels, matches, and lamp)

PAR.Name: x0453
PAR.Comment: EXPENDITURE :energy ( firewood and charcoal) - Vat is not applicable

PAR.Name: x071
PAR.Comment: Expenditure on vehicle and spare parts

PAR.Name: x0561
PAR.Comment: EXPENDITURE : light (batteies, candels, matches, and lamp)

PAR.Name: x0453

PAR.Name: x0111
PAR.Comment: EXPENDITURE : cereals, pulses, and tubers- Vat is not applicable

PAR.Name: x073
PAR.Comment: EXPENDITURE : Transport- Vat is not applicable

PAR.Name: x05621
PAR.Comment: Expenditure on domestic workes Vat is not applicable

PAR.Name: x061
PAR.Comment: Expenditure on Health- Vat is not applicable

PAR.Name: x071
PAR.Comment: Expenditure on vehicle and spare parts

PAR.Name: x10
PAR.Comment: Expenditure on education- Vat is not applicable

PAR.Name: x122
PAR.Comment: Expenditure on financial services- Vat is not applicable

PAR.Name: x0454
PAR.Comment: EXPENDITURE : charcoal and firewood- Vat is not applicable

PAR.Name: x0722
PAR.Comment: EXPENDITURE : fuel- Vat is not applicable

PAR.Name: Comp_Cond
PAR.Comment: If formal sector and business profit tax less that 2.5% of turnover

PAR.Name: Elig_Cond
PAR.Comment: If formal sector and turnover lower than presumptive tax upper limit

SYS_PAR.Value: (les=1 | les=2 | les=3) & ( (i_lindi01!=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) ) | (i_lindi01=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) & ( dhh=1 & (yag=0 | (yag>0 & lfo=1) ) ) ) )
(les=1 | les=2 | les=3) & ( (i_lindi01!=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) ) | (i_lindi01=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) & ( dhh=1 & (yag=0 | (yag>0 & lfo=1) ) ) ) )
(les=1 | les=2 | les=3) & ( (i_lindi01!=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) ) | (i_lindi01=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) & ( dhh=1 & (yag=0 | (yag>0 & lfo=1) ) ) ) )
(les=1 | les=2 | les=3) & ( (i_lindi01!=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) ) | (i_lindi01=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) & ( dhh=1 & (yag=0 | (yag>0 & lfo=1) ) ) ) )
(les=1 | les=2 | les=3) & ( (i_lindi01!=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) ) | (i_lindi01=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) & ( dhh=1 & (yag=0 | (yag>0 & lfo=1) ) ) ) )
(les=1 | les=2 | les=3) & ( (i_lindi01!=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) ) | (i_lindi01=1 & ( yem>0 | yse>0) & ( dhh=1 & (yag=0 | (yag>0 & lfo=1) ) ) ) )
